title: ShopBazaar
subtitle: Redesigning mobile commerce for clarity, speed, and trust
overview: ShopBazaar is a Website e-commerce concept designed to reduce friction in product discovery and checkout. The project demonstrates end-to-end UX thinking—from research and personas to high-fidelity prototypes.

problem.title: Problem
problem.content: Mobile shoppers frequently abandon carts due to cluttered interfaces, unclear pricing, and complex checkout flows. Existing apps prioritize promotions over discoverability, creating cognitive overload during purchase decisions.
bullets: High cart abandonment on mobile commerce apps
Poor product discoverability in category-heavy catalogs
Inconsistent visual hierarchy across shopping flows

research.title: Research
research.content: I conducted qualitative interviews with 8 university students and analyzed 5 leading e-commerce apps. Research focused on browsing behavior, filter usage, and checkout anxiety triggers.
bullets: 8 user interviews (ages 18–24)
5-app competitive audit
Journey mapping for first-time and repeat buyers

insights.title: Insights
insights.content: Users want fast scanning, transparent pricing, and progressive disclosure—not endless promotional banners. Trust signals and saved preferences significantly reduce checkout hesitation.
bullets: 72% of participants preferred card-based product layouts
Users scan price + rating before images on mobile
One-tap reorder was the most requested feature

personas.title: Personas
personas.content: Two primary personas guided design decisions: Priya, a budget-conscious student who shops weekly, and Arjun, a tech enthusiast who researches extensively before buying.
bullets: Priya — Value seeker, mobile-first, time-constrained
Arjun — Research-driven, compares specs and reviews
Both need clear navigation and minimal steps to purchase

userJourney.title: User Journey
userJourney.content: Mapped the journey from app open → browse → product detail → cart → payment → confirmation. Identified three critical drop-off points: search results, size selection, and payment method entry.
bullets: Awareness: Social ads and peer recommendations
Consideration: Filter by price, brand, and ratings
Purchase: Streamlined checkout with saved addresses

competitiveAnalysis.title: Competitive Analysis
competitiveAnalysis.content: Analyzed Amazon, Flipkart, Meesho, Ajio, and Nykaa. Patterns emerged around hero banners (clutter), bottom navigation (effective), and wishlist placement (underutilized on some apps).
bullets: Best-in-class: persistent search + bottom nav
Opportunity: cleaner product cards with inline CTAs
Gap: personalized home feeds without overwhelming promos

wireframes.title: Wireframes
wireframes.content: Low-fidelity wireframes explored three home screen variants and two checkout flows. Iteration focused on information density and thumb-zone placement for primary actions.
bullets: Home: category chips + curated product rails
PLP: grid layout with quick-filter chips
Checkout: 3-step flow with progress indicator

designDecisions.title: Design Decisions
designDecisions.content: Chose an 8pt grid, 16px base type scale, and indigo accent for CTAs. Glassmorphism used sparingly on overlays. Accessibility: 4.5:1 contrast on all text, 44px minimum touch targets.
bullets: Card-based product layout for scannability
Sticky bottom CTA on product detail pages
Skeleton loaders for perceived performance

finalScreens.title: Final Screens
finalScreens.content: High-fidelity screens cover onboarding, home, search, product detail, cart, checkout, and order tracking. A cohesive design system ensures consistency across 24+ screens.
bullets: Home with personalized recommendations
Product detail with reviews and size guide
Order success with tracking timeline

prototype.title: Prototype
prototype.content: Interactive Figma prototype demonstrates the complete purchase flow with micro-interactions: add-to-cart animation, filter transitions, and checkout step progression.
bullets: Clickable prototype with 12 connected flows
Smart animate transitions between states
Usability tested with 3 participants

impact.title: Impact
impact.content: Usability testing showed improved task completion rates and reduced time-on-task for checkout. Participants rated navigation clarity 4.2/5 and visual appeal 4.5/5.
bullets: Checkout flow reduced from 6 to 3 steps
Task completion improved by 35% in testing
Positive feedback on visual hierarchy and spacing

learnings.title: Learnings
learnings.content: Early wireframe testing saved significant rework. Designing for thumb zones and progressive disclosure matters more than visual novelty on mobile commerce.
bullets: Test wireframes before high-fidelity polish
Constraints improve design decisions
Document design tokens early for consistency
